message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it; for a code change the named regression test must DISCRIMINATE — fail if the fix were reverted). Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix G-020 in src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py
What: make_gate_node raises a clear GateError on recorder failure instead of returning the update (no graph-state/trail divergence).
Test(s): test_gate_node_recorder_failure_raises_not_silently_diverges
Steps: read the actual changed file(s); read the test and judge whether it discriminates; check no regression. For a doc/refactor item, judge correctness/coverage rather than revert-discrimination. Verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested). Be skeptical and specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

7dccfd8 fix(hardening-p4): batch E — egress, error contracts, PII & test discrimination (8 findings)
2b07dfd fix(hardening-p3): batch 2 — security + observability seams (8 fixes, 1 FP)
1389242 harden/pass-1: 22 production-readiness fixes (assess→2-agent verify→fix→2-agent confirm) (#123)

0f69735 feat(orchestration): goal-agnostic graph + gate interrupt/resume wiring (#59)

1	"""Human-in-the-loop gate node — the interrupt/resume protocol (GOVERNANCE.md).
2	
3	:func:`make_gate_node` turns an engine :class:`~assay_engine.orchestration.gates.Gate` plus a
4	proposal builder into a LangGraph node that pauses the run at the gate (``interrupt``),
5	surfaces a proposal to the operator, and on resume validates and records the decision.
6	
7	The **gate-correlation guard** rejects a resumed decision aimed at a *different* gate than the
8	one currently parked — a stale or misrouted decision (e.g. delivered late through the
9	governance channel) must not be applied to whatever gate happens to be waiting. The guard's
10	expected gate is the gate the operator named in the resume value, compared against this node's
11	gate; auto-deriving it from the parked gate would make the guard always match and thus a no-op.
12	
13	Two further hardened behaviors, ported faithfully from the prior platform's gate node:
14	
15	- **Re-prompt on a bad resume (not raise).** ``interrupt()`` consumes and *persists* the
16	  resume value into the checkpoint *before* the node returns, so raising on a malformed or
17	  mis-correlated value would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever — the gate
18	  would be permanently bricked. Instead the node validates inside a loop and re-fires
19	  ``interrupt()`` (carrying the error) on a bad value, so the operator can re-submit and
20	  actually recover.
21	- **Terminal-decision idempotency.** An ``approved`` decision is terminal: re-entering an
22	  already-approved gate is a no-op (it does not re-prompt or re-record). ``rejected`` and
23	  ``deferred`` are deliberately NOT terminal, so a reject→revise→re-review loop re-fires the
24	  interrupt as intended.
25	
26	The node returns a partial state update appending one record to ``gate_decisions``; a study's
27	graph state should declare ``gate_decisions`` with a list/add reducer so records accumulate
28	across gates. The decision/recording validator (:func:`apply_gate_decision`) is pure and
29	tested offline; only the ``interrupt`` call requires LangGraph (the optional ``orchestration``
30	extra, imported lazily).
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	import warnings
36	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
37	
38	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
39	
40	GateProposal = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], Mapping[str, Any]]
41	
42	_VALID_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved", "rejected", "deferred"})
43	
44	
45	def apply_gate_decision(gate: Gate, resumed: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
46	    """Validate a resumed operator decision and return the state update to record it.
47	
48	    Raises :class:`GateError` if the resume value is malformed, targets a different gate
49	    (correlation guard), or carries an unrecognized decision.
50	    """
51	    if not isinstance(resumed, Mapping):
52	        raise GateError(
53	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resume value must be a mapping, got {type(resumed).__name__}"
54	        )
55	    target = resumed.get("gate_id")
56	    if target != gate.name:
57	        raise GateError(
58	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: resumed decision targets {target!r} — stale or misrouted; "
59	            "rejecting (gate-correlation guard)"
60	        )
61	    decision = str(resumed.get("decision", "")).strip().lower()
62	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
63	        raise GateError(
64	            f"gate {gate.name!r}: invalid decision {decision!r} "
65	            f"(expected one of {sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)})"
66	        )
67	    # A rejection/deferral with no rationale is governance-relevant; record a sentinel rather
68	    # than a blank (incl. whitespace-only) so the audit trail is never silently empty.
69	    rationale = str(resumed.get("rationale") or "").strip() or "No rationale provided."
70	    record = {"gate": gate.name, "decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
71	    return {"gate_decisions": [record]}
72	
73	
74	# Only an approval is terminal: a re-entered approved gate is a no-op, but a rejected/deferred
75	# gate must re-fire its interrupt so a revise→re-review loop can re-prompt the operator.
76	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = frozenset({"approved"})
77	
78	
79	def already_decided(state: Mapping[str, Any], gate_name: str) -> bool:
80	    """True if ``gate_name`` already holds a terminal (approved) decision in ``gate_decisions``."""
81	    for record in state.get("gate_decisions", []) or []:
82	        if record.get("gate") == gate_name and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS:
83	            return True
84	    return False
85	
86	
87	GateRecorder = Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], None]
88	
89	
90	def make_gate_node(
91	    gate: Gate,
92	    propose: GateProposal,
93	    *,
94	    recorder: GateRecorder | None = None,
95	) -> Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], dict[str, Any]]:
96	    """Build a LangGraph node that parks at ``gate`` for an operator decision.
97	
98	    ``propose(state)`` returns the proposal payload surfaced to the operator (what the gate is
99	    asking them to approve). The node returns a ``{"gate_decisions": [record]}`` update, or
100	    ``{}`` (no-op) if the gate is already terminally approved.
101	
102	    ``recorder`` (optional) bridges each validated decision into the hash-chained provenance
103	    trail (#111): the durable LangGraph path otherwise records gate decisions only as
104	    (untamper-evident) graph state, so the documented append-only trail would miss them. Pass a
105	    callback such as ``lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r)``
106	    so the graph path's approvals/rejections land in the same chained trail as the runner's.
107	
108	    Omitting ``recorder`` is a GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap: gate decisions then live only in
109	    (non-tamper-evident) LangGraph state, never in the hash-chained trail, so a checkpoint edit
110	    flipping ``rejected``→``approved`` is undetectable by ``verify_records``. Because that
111	    insecure configuration is effortless to ship by accident, building a node without a recorder
112	    emits a warning rather than failing silently (pass 3, #F-007).
113	    """
114	    if recorder is None:
115	        warnings.warn(
116	            f"make_gate_node({gate.name!r}) built with no recorder: gate decisions will NOT be "
117	            "written to the hash-chained provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap) — pass a "
118	            "recorder that calls trail.record('gate', ...) for a tamper-evident audit trail",
119	            stacklevel=2,
120	        )
121	
122	    def node(state: Mapping[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
123	        if already_decided(state, gate.name):
124	            return {}  # terminal decision exists — do not re-prompt or re-record
125	        try:
126	            from langgraph.types import interrupt
127	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
128	            raise RuntimeError(
129	                "orchestration requires the 'orchestration' extra (langgraph) — not installed"
130	            ) from exc
131	        proposal = dict(propose(state))
132	        current: dict[str, Any] = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal}
133	        # Validate inside a loop: on a bad/mis-correlated resume, re-fire interrupt (next
134	        # resume index) carrying the error rather than raising — a raise would strand the
135	        # bad value in the checkpoint and replay it forever, bricking the gate.
136	        while True:
137	            resumed = interrupt(current)
138	            try:
139	                update = apply_gate_decision(gate, resumed)
140	            except GateError as exc:
141	                current = {"gate_id": gate.name, "proposal": proposal, "error": str(exc)}
142	                continue
143	            if recorder is not None:
144	                # Record to the trail BEFORE returning the state update (#G-020): the validated
145	                # decision must land in the hash-chained trail and the graph state atomically. If
146	                # the recorder fails, surface a clear GateError INSTEAD of returning the update —
147	                # so the decision is neither half-applied (in graph state but absent from the
148	                # tamper-evident trail) nor silently lost; the operator/runner sees an explicit
149	                # failure to durably record the approval rather than a quietly diverging trail.
150	                try:
151	                    for rec in update.get("gate_decisions", []):
152	                        recorder(rec)
153	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 - normalize any recorder backend failure
154	                    raise GateError(
155	                        f"gate {gate.name!r}: decision validated but could NOT be recorded to the "
156	                        f"provenance trail ({type(exc).__name__}: {exc}); not applying the state "
157	                        "update so graph state cannot diverge from the trail"
158	                    ) from exc
159	            return update
160	
161	    return node
162

1	"""Orchestration graph wiring + gate-node interrupt/resume protocol (offline, langgraph mocked)."""
2	
3	import sys
4	import types
5	
6	import pytest
7	
8	from assay_engine.orchestration.gatenode import apply_gate_decision, make_gate_node
9	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import Gate, GateError
10	from assay_engine.orchestration.graph import RECURSION_LIMIT, compile_graph, resume, run
11	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase
12	
13	
14	def _gate() -> Gate:
15	    return Gate(
16	        "gate_2",
17	        Phase.PREREGISTER,
18	        Phase.CONFIRM,
19	        lambda ctx: __import__(
20	            "assay_engine.orchestration.gates", fromlist=["GateDecision"]
21	        ).GateDecision(approved=True, gate="gate_2", reason="ok"),
22	    )
23	
24	
25	# ---- apply_gate_decision (pure) ----
26	
27	
28	def test_apply_gate_decision_records_valid():
29	    upd = apply_gate_decision(
30	        _gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "Approved", "rationale": "looks good"}
31	    )
32	    assert upd == {
33	        "gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "looks good"}]
34	    }
35	
36	
37	def test_apply_gate_decision_correlation_guard_rejects_other_gate():
38	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="correlation guard"):
39	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_5", "decision": "approved"})
40	
41	
42	def test_apply_gate_decision_rejects_non_mapping():
43	    with pytest.raises(GateError):
44	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), "approved")
45	
46	
47	def test_apply_gate_decision_rejects_unknown_decision():
48	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="invalid decision"):
49	        apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "maybe"})
50	
51	
52\[REDACTED]("d", ["approved", "rejected", "deferred"])
53	def test_apply_gate_decision_accepts_all_verdicts(d):
54	    upd = apply_gate_decision(_gate(), {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": d})
55	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == d
56	
57	
58	# ---- fake langgraph injection ----
59	
60	
61\[REDACTED]
62	def fake_langgraph(monkeypatch):
63	    captured: dict = {"payloads": [], "resumes": []}
64	
65	    def fake_interrupt(payload):
66	        captured["payloads"].append(payload)
67	        if not captured["resumes"]:
68	            raise AssertionError("interrupt called more times than resumes provided")
69	        return captured["resumes"].pop(0)
70	
71	    class Command:
72	        def __init__(self, resume=None):
73	            self.resume = resume
74	
75	    class StateGraph:
76	        def __init__(self, schema):
77	            captured["schema"] = schema
78	            self.nodes = []
79	
80	        def add_node(self, name, fn):
81	            self.nodes.append(name)
82	
83	        def compile(self, **kw):
84	            captured["compile_kw"] = kw
85	            return ("compiled", tuple(self.nodes))
86	
87	    lg = types.ModuleType("langgraph")
88	    types_mod = types.ModuleType("langgraph.types")
89	    types_mod.interrupt = fake_interrupt
90	    types_mod.Command = Command
91	    graph_mod = types.ModuleType("langgraph.graph")
92	    graph_mod.StateGraph = StateGraph
93	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph", lg)
94	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph.types", types_mod)
95	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "langgraph.graph", graph_mod)
96	    return captured
97	
98	
99	# ---- make_gate_node ----
100	
101	
102	def test_make_gate_node_warns_when_no_recorder():
103	    # #F-007: building a gate node without a recorder is a GOVERNANCE §3 audit gap (decisions
104	    # live only in non-tamper-evident graph state). The insecure config must warn, not pass
105	    # silently.
106	    with pytest.warns(UserWarning, match="audit gap"):
107	        make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
108	
109	
110	def test_make_gate_node_with_recorder_does_not_warn(recwarn):
111	    make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {}, recorder=lambda r: None)
112	    assert not [w for w in recwarn.list if "audit gap" in str(w.message)]
113	
114	
115	def test_gate_node_interrupts_with_proposal_and_records(fake_langgraph):
116	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [{"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}]
117	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {"hypotheses": state.get("n", 0)})
118	    upd = node({"n": 3})
119	    assert fake_langgraph["payloads"][0] == {"gate_id": "gate_2", "proposal": {"hypotheses": 3}}
120	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
121	
122	
123	def test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail(fake_langgraph):
124	    # #111: a recorder lands the durable-path gate decision in the hash-chained provenance trail
125	    from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail, verify_records
126	
127	    trail = ProvenanceTrail()
128	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [{"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}]
129	    node = make_gate_node(
130	        _gate(),
131	        lambda state: {},
132	        recorder=lambda r: trail.record("gate", f"gate {r['gate']}: {r['decision']}", **r),
133	    )
134	    node({})
135	    verify_records(trail.entries)  # the bridged decision is in an intact hash chain
136	    gates = [e for e in trail.entries if e.kind == "gate"]
137	    assert gates and gates[0].payload["decision"] == "approved"
138	
139	
140	def test_gate_node_recorder_failure_raises_not_silently_diverges(fake_langgraph):
141	    # #G-020: if the recorder (provenance write) fails, the node must raise a clear GateError and
142	    # NOT return the state update — so the decision can't be applied to graph state while absent
143	    # from the tamper-evident trail (a silent divergence).
144	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [{"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"}]
145	
146	    def boom_recorder(rec):
147	        raise RuntimeError("trail write failed")
148	
149	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {}, recorder=boom_recorder)
150	    with pytest.raises(GateError, match="could NOT be recorded"):
151	        node({})
152	
153	
154	def test_gate_node_reprompts_on_bad_resume_then_recovers(fake_langgraph):
155	    # issue #G1: a mis-correlated then malformed resume must re-fire interrupt, not raise/brick
156	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [
157	        {"gate_id": "gate_9", "decision": "approved"},  # wrong gate -> re-prompt
158	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "huh"},  # invalid decision -> re-prompt
159	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "ok"},  # good
160	    ]
161	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
162	    upd = node({})
163	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
164	    assert len(fake_langgraph["payloads"]) == 3
165	    assert "error" in fake_langgraph["payloads"][1]  # re-prompt carried the correlation error
166	    assert "error" in fake_langgraph["payloads"][2]  # and the invalid-decision error
167	
168	
169	def test_gate_node_noop_when_already_approved(fake_langgraph):
170	    # issue #G2: re-entering an approved gate is a no-op (no interrupt, no duplicate record)
171	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = []  # interrupt must NOT be called
172	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
173	    upd = node({"gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "x"}]})
174	    assert upd == {}
175	    assert fake_langgraph["payloads"] == []
176	
177	
178	def test_gate_node_reprompts_when_previously_rejected(fake_langgraph):
179	    # rejected is NOT terminal — the gate must re-prompt so a revise->re-review loop works
180	    fake_langgraph["resumes"] = [
181	        {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "approved", "rationale": "now ok"}
182	    ]
183	    node = make_gate_node(_gate(), lambda state: {})
184	    upd = node({"gate_decisions": [{"gate": "gate_2", "decision": "rejected", "rationale": "no"}]})
185	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["decision"] == "approved"
186	    assert len(fake_langgraph["payloads"]) == 1
187	
188	
189\[REDACTED]("rationale", [None, "", "   ", "\t\n"])
190	def test_blank_rationale_defaults_to_sentinel(rationale):
191	    payload = {"gate_id": "gate_2", "decision": "rejected"}
192	    if rationale is not None:
193	        payload["rationale"] = rationale
194	    upd = apply_gate_decision(_gate(), payload)
195	    assert upd["gate_decisions"][0]["rationale"] == "No rationale provided."
196	
197	
198	# ---- compile_graph / run / resume ----

content.text: Interesting — the test FAILS as currently committed. The RuntimeError is not being wrapped in a GateError. Let me look more carefully. The traceback shows the failure at line 151 `recorder(rec)` raising RuntimeError directly, which means the try/except is NOT catching it. content.text:
File restored (no diff vs HEAD), all 23 tests pass. 

Now, the discrimination assessment. The test asserts the recorder failure produces a `GateError` matching "could NOT be recorded". Consider the two failure modes the fix prevents:
1. Raw exception propagating (recorder called, no wrap) — test fails (wrong exception type/message). ✓ discriminates
2. Returning the update silently (the actual named divergence bug) — test fails (no exception raised at all). ✓ discriminates

One subtle point: the test only asserts an exception is raised, not that the update is *withheld*. But raising a GateError instead of returning inherently means the update is not returned, so it does cover the core invariant. The fix also correctly records BEFORE returning, and the recorder is invoked inside the try. The `BLE001` broad-except is appropriate here (normalizing any backend failure) and idiomatic. No regression: all sibling tests still pass, including the bridge-success test and re-prompt tests.

Let me also confirm the broad `except Exception` doesn't accidentally swallow the `GateError` from `apply_gate_decision` (it shouldn't — that's in a separate try above the recorder block). Confirmed from the source: `apply_gate_decision` is in its own try/except at lines 138-142; the recorder try/except is separate.

input.reasoning:
Verified fix in /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/orchestration/gatenode.py (lines 143-159). The recorder is invoked inside a try/except BEFORE returning the state update; on any recorder failure it raises a GateError ("...decision validated but could NOT be recorded to the provenance trail...; not applying the state update so graph state cannot diverge from the trail") chained from the original via `from exc`, instead of returning `update`. This correctly closes the named divergence: the decision is neither half-applied (graph state without trail) nor silently lost. The broad `except Exception` (BLE001, justified to normalize any backend failure) does not over-catch — the apply_gate_decision GateError is handled by a separate earlier try/except (lines 138-142), and the recorder try only wraps the recorder loop.

Appropriateness: correct, idiomatic, exception chaining preserved, record-before-return ordering matches the fix intent. No regression — all 23 tests in tests/test_orchestration_graph.py pass, including the success-path bridge test (test_gate_node_bridges_decision_into_provenance_trail) and the re-prompt/no-op tests.

Discrimination: test_gate_node_recorder_failure_raises_not_silently_diverges (tests/test_orchestration_graph.py:140-151) injects a boom_recorder that raises RuntimeError and asserts pytest.raises(GateError, match="could NOT be recorded"). I confirmed the test PASSES on the committed code (after clearing a stale .pyc that initially caused a misleading failure) and FAILS when the try/except wrap is removed (the raw RuntimeError propagates, not matching the GateError type/message). It also discriminates against the alternative pre-fix behavior of silently returning the update (no exception raised → pytest.raises fails). The assertion on exception type+message is sufficient because raising instead of returning inherently withholds the divergent update.

content.text: CONFIRMED. The G-020 fix is both appropriate and complete, and the named regression test discriminates (passes on the fix, fails on revert). Note: I cleaned up a stale `__pycache__` that initially produced a misleading test failure; the working tree was left identical to HEAD.
